hypothesis: If automated scientific information extraction uses a single-agent pipeline that converts PDFs to structure-preserving markdown and integrates GPT-4o-generated image captions prior to extraction with GPT-4.1/5, then it will achieve higher per-column F1 scores and better output-format compliance than GPT-5/Thinking baselines, a general-purpose ChatGPT agent, and domain-specific multi-agent systems on ChemX nanozyme and chelate complex datasets because structured normalization and multimodal cues reduce parsing and schema-adherence errors.
